**Name: ABDOUL S. TOURE JR**

**ID: 26161**

**Course Title: SOFTWARE ENGINEERING COMPREHENSIVE PRATICE**

**Course Code: CSENG 402**

**Date: FEBRUARY 23,2026**

FULL IMPLEMENTATION PLAN

Portfolio Website Development Using Agile Model

Course: Software Engineering Comprehensive Practice (CSENG 402)

Student: Abdoul S. Toure Jr

Client: C. Kima S.J. Wea (DOB: October 18, 2000)

Submission Date: March 31, 2026

1\. INTRODUCTION

1.1 Project Overview

This project involves the design and development of a professional portfolio website for C. Kima S.J. Wea. The system serves as a centralized digital hub to showcase the client's biography, technical skills, and past projects. Developed using Next.js, SQLite, Prisma ORM, and Tailwind CSS, the project adheres to the Agile Software Development Model to ensure flexibility and alignment with client expectations.

1.2 Purpose of the System

• Professional Branding: Establish a unique digital identity.

• Skill Visualization: Categorize and display technical proficiencies.

• Lead Generation: Provide a secure contact channel for recruiters and collaborators.

• Data Persistence: Store visitor inquiries in a structured local database.

2\. REQUIREMENT ENGINEERING

2.1 User Stories (Agile Methodology)

• As a Visitor: I want to view Kima's professional background so that I can understand his expertise.

• As a Recruiter: I want to view specific projects to verify technical claims and coding style.

• As a Client: I want a responsive interface so that my portfolio looks professional on both mobile and desktop devices.

• As a Visitor: I want to submit a contact form so that I can send inquiries directly to the client.

2.2 Functional Requirements

• Display: Profile, biography, skills, and project gallery.

• Interaction: Functional contact form with server-side validation.

• Persistence: Message storage in SQLite via Prisma.

2.3 Non-Functional Requirements

• Performance: Fast loading times using Next.js Server-Side Rendering (SSR).

• Responsiveness: Fluid user interface across all screen sizes using Tailwind CSS.

• Availability: 24/7 uptime through Vercel hosting.

2.4 Scope of the System

In-Scope

• Interactive user interface

• Prisma-driven data layer

• Automated deployment

Out-of-Scope

• User account creation

• Payment systems

• Third-party blog integrations

3\. AGILE IMPLEMENTATION STRATEGY

The project follows an iterative lifecycle divided into four distinct sprints.

- [Phase 0 - Requirement Gathering](./phase-0-requirement-gathering.md)
- [Phase 1 - System Design](./phase-1-system-design.md)
- [Phase 2 - Development / Iteration](./phase-2-development-iteration.md)
- [Phase 3 - Testing / Quality Assurance](./phase-3-testing-qa.md)
- [Phase 4 - Feedback / Evaluation](./phase-4-feedback-evaluation.md)

Sprint 1 - Foundation: Environment setup, Git initialization, and Prisma schema definition.

Sprint 2 - Core UI: Hero section, biography section, and responsive skill cards.

Sprint 3 - Backend Logic: SQLite integration, API routes for contact form, and validation.

Sprint 4 - QA & Deployment: Cross-browser testing, Vercel deployment, and client sign-off.

4\. PROJECT PLAN

The project plan outlines the phases, duration, milestones, and status of the portfolio website development.

| Duration            | Phase                       | Milestone                                                                                    | Status      |
| ------------------- | --------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------- |
| February            | Requirement Gathering       | Project overview, user stories, functional and non-functional requirements                   | Completed   |
| March 1 - March 3   | System Design               | Flowchart, Wireframe, Use Case Diagram, ER Diagram                                           | Completed   |
| March 8 - March 16  | Development / Iteration     | Sprint 1: Environment setup, Git init, Prisma schema<br>Sprint 2: UI development (hero, bio, skills)<br>Sprint 3: API routes, validation, SQLite integration<br>Sprint 4: QA, build, deployment | Completed   |
| March 22 - March 23 | Testing / Quality Assurance | Unit testing, integration testing, system testing, cross-browser testing                     | Completed   |
| March 29 - March 31 | Feedback / Evaluation       | Client review, faculty evaluation, final improvements, deployment to Vercel                 | Completed   |

5. IMPLEMENTATION PHASES DETAILS

5.1 Lesson Plan: Phase 0 - Requirement Gathering (February)
- Objective: Teach requirements engineering through stakeholder analysis and user story definition.
- Key components: interview scripts, user story cards, functional and non-functional requirement templates.
- Activities:
  - Conduct a mock stakeholder interview.
  - Develop at least 6 user stories for the portfolio site.
  - Create a requirements document with clearly identified scope.
- Assessment:
  - Peer validation of the requirement document.
  - Instructor review of user stories for completeness and clarity.
- Status: Completed

5.2 Lesson Plan: Phase 1 - System Design (March 1-3)
- Objective: Teach modeling and UI planning for solution architecture.
- Key components: system diagram, wireframes, use-case diagram, ER diagram.
- Activities:
  - Draft system architecture and data model diagrams.
  - Create wireframes for all main pages and features.
  - Map the data model to Prisma schema fields.
- Assessment:
  - Compare design artifacts to requirement document.
  - Instructor grading of model completeness and consistency.
- Status: Completed

5.3 Lesson Plan: Phase 2 - Development / Iteration (March 8-16)
- Objective: Teach agile implementation, sprint planning, and full-stack coding.
- Key components: Next.js setup, component development, API route coding, Prisma integration.
- Activities:
  - Set up project scaffolding and commit to Git.
  - Build frontend components (hero, about, skills, contact form).
  - Implement `/api/contact` route and connect to Prisma/SQLite.
  - Execute iterative testing and bug fixes.
- Assessment:
  - Code review by instructor and passes of automated tests.
  - Demonstration of contact flow writing to database.
- Status: Completed

5.4 Lesson Plan: Phase 3 - Testing / Quality Assurance (March 22-23)
- Objective: Teach testing methodologies and quality assurance workflows.
- Key components: unit tests, integration tests, cross-browser checks.
- Activities:
  - Write tests for form validation and API handler logic.
  - Perform manual UI tests across mobile and desktop viewports.
  - Log and resolve defects with version-controlled fixes.
- Assessment:
  - Test report with coverage and issue resolution.
  - Sign-off on acceptance criteria completion.
- Status: Completed

5.5 Lesson Plan: Phase 4 - Feedback / Evaluation (March 29-31)
- Objective: Teach deployment, feedback iteration, and final evaluation.
- Key components: Vercel deployment, client review, documentation updates.
- Activities:
  - Deploy project to Vercel and verify live URL.
  - Collect client and faculty feedback; implement final enhancements.
  - Document project delivery and lesson review.
- Assessment:
  - Final presentation with live demo and project docs.
  - Grading checklist against all phase goals.
- Status: Completed
- Status: Completed

6\. SYSTEM ARCHITECTURE & DATA MODEL

6.1 Technical Stack

Framework: Next.js (Full-stack capabilities)

Database: SQLite (Lightweight file-based database)

ORM: Prisma (Type-safe database interaction)

Styling: Tailwind CSS (Utility-first responsive design)

6.2 Data Schema (Prisma Model)

model Message {

id Int @id @default(autoincrement())

name String

email String

message String

createdAt DateTime @default(now())

}

7\. TESTING & RISK MITIGATION

7.1 Testing Strategy

• Unit Testing: Validating React components and utility functions.

• Integration Testing: Verifying the data flow between the contact form and SQLite database.

• User Acceptance Testing (UAT): Final review with the client to ensure alignment with requirements.

7.2 Risk Management

• Scope Creep: Controlled by adhering strictly to the defined project scope.

• Database Reliability: Prisma migrations ensure schema consistency.

• Error Handling: Avoid exposing sensitive server details in error messages.

• Spam Protection: Input validation prevents automated spam submissions.

8\. SEARCH ENGINE OPTIMIZATION (SEO)

Search Engine Optimization ensures that the portfolio website can be easily discovered through search engines.

• Performance Optimization: Fast loading pages improve user experience and ranking.

• Mobile Optimization: Responsive design improves mobile search ranking.

• Next.js SEO Features: Server-side rendering enhances indexing.

• Semantic HTML Structure: Proper headings improve readability.

• Meta Tags Optimization: Each webpage includes optimized title and description tags.

9\. SECURITY IMPLEMENTATION

Security measures are necessary to protect the website from unauthorized access and data misuse.

• HTTPS encryption through Vercel deployment

10. ADMIN PANEL AND SECURITY ENHANCEMENTS

• Admin route middleware (`src/middleware.ts`) secures `/admin/*` with an admin key.
• Admin login page (`/admin/login`) sets a cookie `admin_key` for authentication.
• `src/app/api/contact/route.ts` requires `x-admin-key` for GET, PATCH, DELETE requests.
• Data operations: list, delete, and read/unread toggling for messages.

11. API ENDPOINT SUMMARY

• POST /api/contact - save new message (public, form submission)
• GET /api/contact - retrieve messages (admin-only)
• PATCH /api/contact - update message `read` status (admin-only)
• DELETE /api/contact?id=ID - remove a message (admin-only)

12. DEV NOTES

• After schema update run:
  npm run prisma migrate dev --name add-read-flag
• Set env variables:
  ADMIN_KEY=your-admin-key
  NEXT_PUBLIC_ADMIN_KEY=your-admin-key

13. TESTING AND QA

• Manual QA covers form submission, admin login, message listing, toggling read/unread, deleting messages, and middleware protection.
• Add unit/integration tests with preferred framework (Jest or Vitest) for API and admin routes.

14. DEPLOYMENT

• Deploy to Vercel with environment variables set in project settings.
• Verify `/admin/login` and `/admin/messages` are protected after deployment.

15. FINAL HANDOVER

• Include a brief walkthrough video or screenshots in the final project report.
• Provide credential instructions for admin access in deployment notes.


• Input validation on both client and server sides

• Protection against SQL injection through Prisma ORM

• Secure handling of form submissions

• Prevention of sensitive data exposure

10\. CONCLUSION

The portfolio website for C. Kima S.J. Wea has been successfully developed using the Agile Software Development Model. All phases from requirement gathering to deployment have been completed, resulting in a fully functional, responsive, and secure website. The implementation utilized modern technologies including Next.js for the frontend and backend, Prisma ORM for database management, and Tailwind CSS for styling. The project met all functional and non-functional requirements, and was deployed to Vercel for 24/7 availability. This project demonstrates the application of software engineering principles in a real-world scenario, fulfilling the academic requirements of CSENG 402.

11\. LESSON PLANS FOR PHASES

The following lesson plans are designed for teaching software engineering students how to execute each phase of the portfolio website development project. Each plan includes objectives, materials, activities, assessment, and duration to facilitate learning and replication.

**Lesson Plan for Phase 0: Requirement Gathering**

**Title:** Requirements Engineering Fundamentals  
**Objectives:**  
- Understand the importance of requirements gathering in software development.  
- Learn techniques for eliciting user stories and requirements from stakeholders.  
- Differentiate between functional and non-functional requirements.  
- Define project scope and constraints.  

**Materials:**  
- Stakeholder interview templates, user story cards, requirements traceability matrix.  
- Case study: Portfolio website client scenario.  
- Agile methodology resources.  

**Procedure/Activities:**  
1. Introduction (15 min): Explain the role of requirements in preventing project failures.  
2. Stakeholder Analysis (30 min): Students identify stakeholders and plan interviews.  
3. User Story Creation (45 min): Write user stories for the portfolio website.  
4. Requirements Classification (30 min): Categorize functional vs non-functional requirements.  
5. Scope Definition (15 min): Establish in-scope and out-of-scope items.  

**Assessment:**  
- Completed user stories and requirements document.  
- Peer review of requirements clarity and completeness.  

**Duration:** 2 hours 15 minutes.  
**Homework:** Research requirements gathering techniques for different project types.

**Lesson Plan for Phase 1: System Design**

**Title:** System Architecture and Design Principles  
**Objectives:**  
- Learn to create system architecture diagrams.  
- Design user interfaces through wireframes and prototypes.  
- Develop use case and ER diagrams.  
- Select appropriate technologies for project requirements.  

**Materials:**  
- Design tools (Figma, Draw.io), UML diagram templates.  
- Technical stack evaluation criteria.  
- Sample wireframes and diagrams.  

**Procedure/Activities:**  
1. Introduction (15 min): Overview of system design importance.  
2. Architecture Design (30 min): Create high-level system architecture.  
3. UI/UX Design (45 min): Develop wireframes for portfolio website.  
4. Database Design (30 min): Create ER diagrams for data models.  
5. Technology Selection (15 min): Evaluate and choose tech stack.  

**Assessment:**  
- Complete design document with diagrams and wireframes.  
- Technology selection justification report.  

**Duration:** 2 hours 15 minutes.  
**Homework:** Compare different architectural patterns.

**Lesson Plan for Phase 2: Development / Iteration**

**Title:** Agile Development and Implementation  
**Objectives:**  
- Apply Agile sprints for iterative development.  
- Implement responsive user interfaces.  
- Build backend APIs with validation.  
- Integrate databases using ORMs.  

**Materials:**  
- Development environment (VS Code, Node.js), Git repository.  
- Next.js, Tailwind CSS, Prisma documentation.  
- Code review checklists.  

**Procedure/Activities:**  
1. Introduction (15 min): Review Agile sprint methodology.  
2. Sprint Planning (30 min): Break down development into sprints.  
3. Frontend Development (45 min): Build responsive UI components.  
4. Backend Development (45 min): Implement APIs and database integration.  
5. Code Review (15 min): Peer code reviews and refactoring.  

**Assessment:**  
- Functional code implementation meeting sprint goals.  
- Code quality assessment using linting tools.  

**Duration:** 2 hours 30 minutes.  
**Homework:** Research continuous integration practices.

**Lesson Plan for Phase 3: Testing / Quality Assurance**

**Title:** Software Testing and Quality Assurance  
**Objectives:**  
- Understand different types of software testing.  
- Implement unit and integration tests.  
- Perform system testing and validation.  
- Ensure cross-browser compatibility.  

**Materials:**  
- Testing frameworks (Jest, Cypress), browser testing tools.  
- Test case templates, bug tracking system.  
- Code coverage tools.  

**Procedure/Activities:**  
1. Introduction (15 min): Overview of testing pyramid.  
2. Unit Testing (30 min): Write and run unit tests for components.  
3. Integration Testing (30 min): Test API endpoints and data flow.  
4. System Testing (30 min): End-to-end testing of application.  
5. Compatibility Testing (15 min): Verify cross-browser functionality.  

**Assessment:**  
- Test suite with passing tests and coverage report.  
- Bug report documentation.  

**Duration:** 2 hours.  
**Homework:** Study test-driven development (TDD).

**Lesson Plan for Phase 4: Feedback / Evaluation**

**Title:** Project Evaluation and Deployment  
**Objectives:**  
- Conduct client feedback sessions.  
- Perform final project evaluation.  
- Deploy applications to production.  
- Document lessons learned.  

**Materials:**  
- Deployment platforms (Vercel, Netlify), feedback survey templates.  
- Project retrospective guides, documentation tools.  
- Performance monitoring tools.  

**Procedure/Activities:**  
1. Introduction (15 min): Importance of evaluation and feedback.  
2. Client Review (30 min): Present project and gather feedback.  
3. Deployment (30 min): Configure and deploy to hosting platform.  
4. Retrospective (30 min): Analyze project successes and improvements.  
5. Documentation (15 min): Finalize project documentation.  

**Assessment:**  
- Deployed live application.  
- Completed project retrospective report.  

**Duration:** 2 hours.  
**Homework:** Research DevOps and continuous deployment.

**Project Plan**

<div class="joplin-table-wrapper"><table><tbody><tr><th><p><strong>Duration</strong></p></th><th><p><strong>Phase</strong></p></th><th><p><strong>Mile stone</strong></p></th><th><p><strong>Status</strong></p></th></tr><tr><td><p>February</p></td><td><p>Require Gathering</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>March 1 - 3</p></td><td><p>Design</p></td><td><ul><li>Flow chart</li><li>Wire frame</li><li>Use-case Diagrams</li><li>ER-Diagram</li></ul></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>March 8 - 16</p></td><td><p>Development /Iteration</p></td><td><ul><li>Coding</li></ul></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>March 22 - 23</p></td><td><p>Testing / Quality</p></td><td><ul><li>Unit Testing</li><li>System Testing</li></ul></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>March 9 - 30</p></td><td><p>Feedback / Quality</p><ul><li>User</li><li>Faculty</li></ul></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table></div>

**SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (SRS)**

Portfolio Website for C. Kima S.J. Wea

Course: Software Engineering Comprehensive Practice (CSENG 402)

Student: Abdoul S. Toure Jr.

Client: C. Kima S.J. Wea

Technology Stack: Next.js, SQLite, Prisma ORM, Tailwind CSS

Development Model: Agile Model

Submission Date: March 31, 2026

**1\. INTRODUCTION**

**1.1 Purpose**

This Software Requirements Specification (SRS) document provides a detailed description of the requirements for the Portfolio Website System for C. Kima S.J. Wea.

The purpose of this document is to clearly define the system's functional requirements, non-functional requirements, system constraints, and operating environment.

This document will guide the development, testing, and deployment of the portfolio website.

**1.2 Scope**

The Portfolio Website System is a web-based application that allows the client to present personal and professional information online.

The website will allow visitors to view the client's biography, professional skills, explore completed projects, and send messages through a contact form.

The system will store messages submitted by visitors in a database.

The website will provide a professional online presence for the client and improve access to personal and professional information.

**1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, and Abbreviations**

SRS - Software Requirements Specification

SEO - Search Engine Optimization

ORM - Object Relational Mapping

UI - User Interface

UAT - User Acceptance Testing

Agile - Iterative software development model

**1.4 Document Overview**

This document includes sections describing the system overview, functional requirements, non-functional requirements, system architecture, database design, security requirements, and system testing.

**2\. OVERALL DESCRIPTION**

2.1 Product Perspective

The Portfolio Website System is a standalone web application accessible through modern web browsers.

The system will consist of three main components: the frontend interface, backend server, and SQLite database.

The system will be hosted online using modern cloud deployment platforms.

**2.2 Product Functions**

The system will display the client's biography, professional skills, and portfolio projects. It will also allow visitors to send messages and store those messages in the database while providing a responsive interface for different devices.

**2.3 User Classes and Characteristics**

Visitors: Individuals who access the website to learn about the client. They can browse information and send messages.

Client (Website Owner): The owner benefits from professional online representation and receiving messages from visitors.

**2.4 Operating Environment**

Frontend: Next.js

Styling: Tailwind CSS

Database: SQLite

ORM: Prisma

Hosting: Vercel

Browser Support: Chrome, Firefox, Edge

**2.5 Design and Implementation Constraints**

The system must be developed using Next.js, use SQLite as the database, use Prisma ORM, support responsive design, and be deployable on the web.

**2.6 Assumptions and Dependencies**

The client will provide biography and project information. Internet connectivity will be available for deployment, and users will access the website through web browsers.

**3\. EXTERNAL INTERFACE REQUIREMENTS**

**3.1 User Interface**

The system will provide a simple and user-friendly interface with the following pages: Home, Biography, Skills, Projects, and Contact.

The interface will be responsive for both mobile and desktop devices.

**3.2 Software Interface**

The system will interact with Prisma ORM for database operations, SQLite database for message storage, and Next.js for application development.

**3.3 Hardware Interface**

The system will operate on smartphones, tablets, and desktop computers.

**3.4 Communication Interface**

The website will communicate using HTTP/HTTPS protocols, with secure HTTPS communication implemented during deployment.

**4\. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS**

**4.1 Biography Display**

The system shall display the client's biography including personal introduction, educational background, and professional information.

**4.2 Skills Display**

The system shall display the client's professional skills such as programming skills, technical competencies, and other professional abilities.

**4.3 Project Portfolio**

The system shall display projects with a title, description, and technologies used.

**4.4 Contact Form**

The system shall provide a contact form allowing visitors to submit their name, email, subject, and message.

**4.5 Message Storage**

The system shall store submitted messages in the database including name, email, subject, message content, and date/time.

**5\. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS**

**5.1 Performance Requirements**

The system should load pages quickly and respond efficiently to user requests.

**5.2 Usability Requirements**

The system should provide a simple and easy-to-navigate interface.

**5.3 Availability Requirements**

The website should be accessible online at all times.

**5.4 Security Requirements**

Security measures include input validation, protection against SQL injection, and secure HTTPS communication.

**5.5 Responsiveness**

The system must function properly on mobile devices, tablets, and desktop computers.

**6\. DATABASE DESIGN**

The database will store messages from the contact form.

Fields include: name, email, subject, content, and created date/time.

**7\. SYSTEM TESTING**

Unit Testing: Individual components tested separately.

Integration Testing: Contact form interaction with the database tested.

User Acceptance Testing (UAT): Client reviews system before deployment.

**8\. FUTURE ENHANCEMENTS**

Possible improvements include a blog system, analytics integration, LinkedIn integration, and an admin dashboard.

**9\. CONCLUSION**

The Portfolio Website System will provide a professional platform for showcasing the client's achievements and skills online. Using modern technologies and Agile development ensures flexibility, scalability, and efficient system performance.

**SOFTWARE DESIGN DOCUMENT (SDD)**

Portfolio Website for **C. Kima S.J. Wea**

**Course: Software Engineering Comprehensive Practice (CSENG 402)**

Student: Abdoul S. Toure Jr.

Client: C. Kima S.J. Wea

Technology Stack: Next.js, SQLite, Prisma ORM, Tailwind CSS

Development Model: Agile Model

Submission Date: February 2026

**1\. INTRODUCTION**

1.1 Purpose

The purpose of this Software Design Document (SDD) is to describe the architecture and design of the Portfolio Website System for C. Kima S.J. Wea.

This document explains how the system will be implemented based on the requirements defined in the Software Requirements Specification (SRS).

**1.2 Scope**

The Portfolio Website System is designed to provide a professional online platform where visitors can learn about the client's biography, skills, and projects.

The system also allows visitors to send messages through a contact form, and these messages will be stored in a database.

**1.3 Definitions and Acronyms**

SRS - Software Requirements Specification

SDD - Software Design Document

ORM - Object Relational Mapping

UI - User Interface

API - Application Programming Interface

**2\. SYSTEM ARCHITECTURE**

The Portfolio Website System follows a Three-Tier Architecture consisting of the Presentation Layer, Application Layer, and Data Layer.

**Presentation Layer**

This layer manages the user interface of the system.

Technologies Used: Next.js, Tailwind CSS

Functions: Displays website pages and handles user interaction.

**Application Layer**

This layer contains the application logic of the system.

Functions: Processes user requests, handles form submissions, and communicates with the database.

Technology Used: Next.js API Routes

Data Layer

This layer manages and stores system data.

Technologies Used: SQLite Database, Prisma ORM

Functions: Stores visitor messages and retrieves stored data when needed.

**3\. SYSTEM COMPONENT DESIGN**

Home Page Component - Displays welcome message, profile introduction, and navigation menu.

Biography Component - Displays detailed information about the client including education and experience.

Skills Component - Displays professional and technical skills.

Projects Component - Displays portfolio projects completed by the client.

Contact Form Component - Allows visitors to send messages to the client.

**4\. DATABASE DESIGN**

The database stores messages submitted by visitors through the contact form.

Fields: id, name, email, subject, content, createdAt.

Prisma Schema Example:

model Message {

id Int @id @default(autoincrement())

name String

email String

subject String

content String

createdAt DateTime @default(now())

}

**5\. USER INTERFACE DESIGN**

The website includes the following pages:

Home Page - Welcome message and introduction.

Biography Page - Personal and educational information.

Skills Page - Professional competencies.

Projects Page - Portfolio projects with descriptions.

Contact Page - Message submission form.

**6\. SYSTEM FLOW**

1\. A visitor opens the website.

2\. The visitor navigates through different pages.

3\. The visitor fills out the contact form.

4\. The system validates the input.

5\. The message is stored in the SQLite database.

**6\. The system confirms successful message submission.**

**7\. SECURITY DESIGN**

Security measures include input validation, SQL injection protection, secure HTTPS communication, and email format validation.

**8\. DEPLOYMENT DESIGN**

The system will be deployed using Vercel.

Deployment steps: Build the Next.js app, connect to GitHub, deploy on Vercel, and configure the database.

**9\. TESTING STRATEGY**

Unit Testing - Testing individual components.

Integration Testing - Testing interaction between components.

User Acceptance Testing - Client verifies system requirements.

**10\. CONCLUSION**

The SDD describes the architecture and design of the portfolio website system and ensures efficient, scalable, and maintainable implementation using modern technologies.
